steps.title: Find the GCF of the coefficients
steps.content:
To find a factor of the polynomial, identify the greatest common factor (GCF) of all terms. The polynomial $2x^2 + 38x + 10$ has coefficients 2, 38, and 10. All are divisible by 2:
  • The first coefficient is \$2$ itself
  • $38 = 2 \times 19$
  • $10 = 2 \times 5$
Therefore, the GCF is 2.

steps.title: Check for common variable factors
steps.content: The polynomial has three terms: $2x^2$, $38x$, and \$10$. Since the constant term 10 contains no variable, there is no variable that appears in all terms. Therefore, the only common factor is the numerical value 2.

steps.title: Factor the polynomial and identify the answer
steps.content:
Factor out 2 from all terms:
$$2x^2 + 38x + 10 = 2(x^2 + 19x + 5)$$This confirms that 2 is a factor of the polynomial. That matches choice A — A is correct.
